name: Algorithms
author: Recurse
description: Search, sort, recursion, and complexity thinking.

questions.question: What is the time complexity of a standard binary search?
questions.explanation: Each step halves the search space.

questions.question: Complete the base case for recursive merge sort.
questions.explanation: The recursion stops when the list has one item or fewer.

questions.question: What is the flaw in this binary search?
choices: left and right should move past mid, or the loop can stall
mid must always be a float
questions.explanation: If you do not move past mid, the same midpoint can repeat forever.

questions.question: Why are two-pointer solutions often efficient?
choices: They can turn nested scans into a single pass
They only work on sorted data
questions.explanation: Two pointers often let you avoid an inner loop.

sections.title: Complexity first
sections.body: Before you optimize code, ask how the cost grows as the input gets bigger. Big O is the language we use to compare algorithmic tradeoffs.
sections.tip: A slow algorithm on small input can still be the wrong design.

sections.title: Binary search
sections.body: Binary search only works on sorted data. Each step cuts the search space roughly in half, which is why it is so much faster than a linear scan.
sections.tip: If the input is not sorted, binary search is invalid.

sections.title: Sorting and recursion
sections.body: Merge sort splits the data, sorts each half, then merges them. Recursion is just a function solving a smaller version of the same problem until it hits a base case.
sections.tip: Every recursive function needs a base case.

sections.title: Two pointers
sections.body: Two-pointer techniques are useful when you can move from both ends of a structure toward the middle. They often turn an otherwise quadratic problem into a linear one.
sections.tip: Ask what invariant the pointers are maintaining.

keyTerms.definition: A step-by-step procedure for solving a problem.
A search strategy that halves the search space each step.
A technique using two indices to scan data efficiently.

lesson.feynmanPrompt: Explain why binary search fails if the input is not sorted.
